Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»BREAKING: Wayde van Niekerk fails in 200m final bid: Career over?

South Africa’s Wayde van Niekerk exited the men’s 200m at the Paris Olympics 2024 on Wednesday. Here’s what happened …

South Africa’sWayde van Niekerkexited the men’s200mat theParis Olympics 2024on Wednesday.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Running in the third and final semi-final, Van Niekerk crossed the line in a tie for seventh place in a pedestrian20.72.

Only the top two finishers in each of the three semi-finals plus the next two fastest times reached the eight-man final.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

Van Niekerk is the world record holder in the men’s 400m which he set in winning the gold medal at the 2016 Rio de Janeiro Olympics.

As reported byThe South African website, Van Niekerk did not contest the 400m in Paris.

It’s highly likely Van Niekerk, at age 32, has run his final ever individual race at the Olympics.

Meanwhile, South Africa’sShaun Maswanganyi, who ran in the first semi-final, finished fourth in20.42to also miss out on a spot in the final.

Botswana’sLetsile Tebogoposted the fastest time overall of19.96.

The final of the men’s 200m will take place onThursday, 8 Augustat20:30(SA time).
